task_objective: Replace the pre-scope freeze acquisition deadlock with a verified SIGSTOP-only launch gate while preserving cgroup freezing for already-created Steam app scopes.
tests_added: Pre-scope acquisition succeeds as stop-only without polling or SIGCONT
Process stopped-state and child detection fail closed against synthetic proc roots
Stop-only lease renewal, explicit resume, watchdog cleanup, and unload cleanup
Copy-time gate verification rejects missing, mismatched, expired, resumed, or thawed gates
Frontend passes the lease PID and ID through conflict resolution
Log analyzer recognizes stop-only acquisition success
design_decisions: A ScopeNotReadyError after SIGSTOP is a successful era-1 gate only when the PID is verifiably stopped, has no children, and retains the captured identity.
The scope acquisition timeout and polling parameters were removed because waiting for scope creation while holding SIGSTOP is structurally deadlocked.
The pre-scope timeout assertions and wait-for-scope success contracts were inverted because waiting was the buggy behavior; era-2 freeze verification timeout coverage remains unchanged.
The facade signature inventory was updated because gate PID and lease ID are required additions to the conflict-resolution RPC contract.
Restore-backup conflict resolution fails closed with gate_lost, while keep-local remains independent of restore-side gate verification.
Process and lease helpers were extracted to preserve the existing module-size budgets instead of increasing them.

results.automated: Focused backend and frontend RED-GREEN suites passed before final repository quality gates.
results.on_device: Deferred and outstanding. A Steam Deck run must confirm the black-screen hold, restore-before-start behavior, cancellation resume, and plugin-unload resume using a genuine save conflict.
